---
title: "Open Learning 2017"
author: "Daniel Klotz"
output:
  tufte::tufte_html: 
    tufte_variant: "default"
    tufte_features: "fonts"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

In high school I had the epiphany that it is better to follow good teachers then good topics. Throughout my studies I tried to follow that maxim as good as possible, but forgot about it recently. This year I want to re-establish my principle once more.

---

# Books

1. Garrett Grolemund, Hadley Wickham: *R for Data Science*
2. Claus Mattheck: *Die Körpersprache der Bauteile*
3. John M. Chambers: *Extending R*

---

# Videos
## 1 Computing with Data

### 1.1 Hadley Wickham
a. [PLOTCON 2016 - New open viz in R](https://youtu.be/cU0-NrUxRw4)
b. [Data Science with R](https://youtu.be/K-ss_ag2k9E)
c. [Managing many models with R](https://youtu.be/rz3_FDVt9eg)
d. [Data Science in the Tidyverse](https://www.rstudio.com/resources/videos/data-science-in-the-tidyverse/)

### 1.2 Bred Victor
- To Do *TM*

### 1.3 Norman J. Wildberger
- [Algebraic Topology: A beginner's course](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL41FDABC6AA085E78)

### 1.4 Bart Kosko
a. [Negative Evidence](https://www.edge.org/response-detail/27130)
b. Noise (2nd read)

## 2 Misc
[Listening to Music](http://oyc.yale.edu/music/musi-112)
